title: 🚀 Deployment Frequency [v2]

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(
  (SELECT
    CASE mode
      WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data' || COALESCE(' — ' || NULLIF(source_label, ''), '')
      WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data' || COALESCE(' — ' || NULLIF(source_label, ''), '')
      WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment' || COALESCE(' — ' || NULLIF(source_label, ''), '')
      ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
    END
  FROM data_mode ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1),
  '⚙️ Awaiting first sync…'
) AS "Data Source"
FROM (SELECT 1) dummy

options.content:
# 🚀 Deployment Frequency

**DORA Pillar: Throughput**

## What it Measures
Deployment Frequency measures how often your organisation successfully deploys code to production. It is one of the two DORA *throughput* metrics and reflects the pace at which your team delivers value to users.

## Why it Matters
Higher deployment frequency indicates a mature, well-automated CI/CD pipeline and a team that delivers small, incremental changes. Elite-performing teams deploy on demand (multiple times per day), while low performers deploy less than once per month.

## How to Interpret
| Performance Level | Frequency | What it Means |
|---|---|---|
| 🟢 Elite | Multiple deploys/day (>7/wk) | On-demand delivery, small batch sizes |
| 🟡 High | 1–7 deploys/week | Regular cadence, good automation |
| 🟠 Medium | 1–4 deploys/month (<1/wk) | Larger batches, some manual steps |
| 🔴 Low | <1 deploy/month (<0.25/wk) | Infrequent releases, high risk per deploy |

## How to Take Action
- **If low**: Break work into smaller PRs, automate deployment pipelines, reduce manual approval gates
- **If declining**: Investigate CI/CD bottlenecks, review branch protection policies, check for flaky tests blocking deployments
- **If improving**: Document what's working, share practices across teams

panels.description: Deployment Frequency — DORA throughput pillar. Performance tiers and action guidance.

### What it measures
Average number of successful deployments per week in the selected environment and time range.

### What insight it offers
Elite: ≥5/day, High: ≥1/week, Medium: ≥1/month, Low: <1/month. Higher frequency indicates better CI/CD maturity.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Only counts deployments with 'success' status
- Divide total by number of weeks in the time range

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id)::float / GREATEST(1, EXTRACT(DAY FROM ($__timeTo()::timestamptz - $__timeFrom()::timestamptz)) / 7.0) AS "Deployments/Week"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
AND ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.title: Deployment Frequency

panels.description:
### What it measures
Weekly count of successful deployments in the selected environment over time.

### What insight it offers
Shows deployment velocity trends — identify acceleration, slowdowns, or seasonal patterns in your release cadence.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Partial weeks at the start/end of the range will have lower counts
- Environment filter applied

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Deployments per Week

panels.description:
### What it measures
Total successful deployments grouped by environment in the selected time range.

### What insight it offers
Compare deployment volumes across environments to understand where delivery focus lies.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- The environment template variable does not filter this panel; it shows all environments for comparison

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  d.environment AS "Environment",
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC

panels.title: Deployments by Environment

panels.description:
### What it measures
Percentage of deployments in the selected environment that completed successfully.

### What insight it offers
Low success rates indicate pipeline instability or environment configuration issues that need attention.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Deployments with no status record are excluded
- May include retries counted as separate deployments

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN ds.state = 'success' THEN d.deployment_id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id), 0), 1
) AS "Success Rate (%)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.title: Deployment Success Rate

panels.description:
### What it measures
Weekly count of failed deployments in the selected environment over time.

### What insight it offers
Spikes in failures often correlate with risky changes, under-tested releases, or infrastructure issues.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Failures are deployments with state = 'failure' only
- Does not include deployments with state = 'error' or 'cancelled'

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Failed Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Failed Deployments Timeline

panels.description:
### What it measures
Last 50 deployments in the selected environment, ordered by most recent.

### What insight it offers
Provides an audit trail of recent releases including who triggered them and their final status.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Capped at 50 rows for performance
- Status reflects the latest status record; earlier states are not shown

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  d.sha,
  d.environment,
  ds.state AS status,
  d.creator_login,
  d.created_at
FROM deployments d
LEFT JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
ORDER BY d.created_at DESC
LIMIT 50

panels.title: Recent Deployments

panels.description:
### What it measures
Comparison of weekly deployment counts for PRs authored by active Copilot users versus non-Copilot users.

### What insight it offers
If Copilot users ship more frequently, it may indicate reduced friction in the development workflow.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Copilot user definition: active seat in the last 28 days
- Deployment-PR linkage is required; unlinked deployments are excluded

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Deployment Frequency: Copilot vs Non-Copilot

list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment
list.label: Environment
list.name: environment
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment

description: DORA Pillar: Deployment Frequency — Measures how often code is successfully deployed. Elite teams deploy multiple times per day.
